description: This API routine is obsolete. It has been replaced by the API routine facts_data_2, and is implemented by a call to facts_data_2. Use this API to modify the data of an existing FACTS device in the working case or to add a new FACTS device to the working case. This API routine was first introduced in release 28.0.0. ierr, realaro = facts_data(fd, intgar, realari)

parameters.name: fd
parameters.description: Number (which the api converts to a left-justified name) of the facts device for which data is to be specified. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: realari
parameters.description: An array of thirteen elements specifying corresponding to values REALAR(1) through REALAR(13) as described above. (input).

return_values.name: realaro
return_values.description: An array of six elements specifying corresponding to values REALAR(14) through REALAR(19) as described above. (output).

intgar.description: I, sending end bus number, can also be specified with keyword ‘i’.
J, terminal end bus number (0 for STATCON), can also be specified with keyword ‘j’.
MODE, control mode, can also be specified with keyword ‘mode’.
OWNER, owner number, can also be specified with keyword ‘owner’.
VSREF, series voltage reference code for modes 4 and 7 (0 for sending end voltage; 1 for series current), can also be specified with keyword ‘vsref’.

realari.description: PDES, desired real power at terminal bus, can also be specified with keyword ‘pdes’.
QDES, desired reactive power at terminal bus, can also be specified with keyword ‘qdes’.
VSET, sending bus voltage setpoint, can also be specified with keyword ‘vset’.
SHMX, sending end maximum shunt current (MVA at rated voltage), can also be specified with keyword ‘shmax’.
TRMX, maximum bridge real power transfer, can also be specified with keyword ‘trmx’.
VTMN, minimum terminal bus voltage magnitude, can also be specified with keyword ‘vtmn’.
VTMX, maximum terminal bus voltage magnitude, can also be specified with keyword ‘vtmx’.
VSMX, maximum series voltage magnitude, can also be specified with keyword ‘vsmx’.
IMX, maximum series current (MVA at rated voltage), can also be specified with keyword ‘imx’.
LINX, pu reactance of temporary series element, can also be specified with keyword ‘linx’.
RMPCT, percent of contributed reactive power, can also be specified with keyword ‘rmpct’.
SET1, real component of constant Z (mode = 3), magnitude of constant V (mode = 4), or VD (mode = 7 or 8), can also be specified with keyword ‘set1’.
SET2, imaginary component of constant Z (mode = 3), angle of constant V (mode = 4), or VQ (mode = 7 or 8), can also be specified with keyword ‘set2’.

realaro.description: real component of present effective series Z (returned).
imaginary component of present effective series Z (returned).
magnitude of present series voltage, FDVREF reference (returned).
le of present series voltage, FDVREF reference (returned).
real component of present series voltage, FDVREF reference (returned).
imaginary component of present series voltage, FDVREF reference (returned).

error_codes.description: no sending end bus is specified.
the same bus is specified for the sending and terminal end buses.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.

warnings - one or more of: - Invalid FACTS device control model. - Invalid owner number. - Invalid series voltage reference code. - Shunt element voltage setpoint must be positive. - Terminal bus voltage limit must be positive. - VTMX is less than VTMN. - Series voltage limit must be positive. - Shunt current limit must not be negative. - Bridge real power transfer limit must not be negative. - Series current limit must not be negative. - Series reactance must be positive. - Shunt current limit is not 0.0 for an IPFC device. - Bridge real power transfer limit is not 0.0 for an IPFC device. - Impedance setpoint has reactance of 0.0. - RMPCT is not positive. - Master IPFC FACTS device not found. - Bus is dummy bus of a multi- section line. - Multi-section line grouping deleted. - Owner table is full.
